food_subgroup: Fried chicken and potato combo meal

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a combined chicken pops and seasoned fries combo meal. FOODON_00002490 (fried chicken food product) is used as the closest parent for the chicken component. The fries component is not separately anchored.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FAN (Chicken meat, fried) is used as the closest parent. No FoodEx2 code exists for a combined fried chicken bites and seasoned fries combo meal.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific combo meal product.

serving.description: 1 combo (fries portion + chicken pops portion with seasoning)
serving.natural_language_portion: One Tera Chicken Pops Combo serving at Potato Corner consists of a large portion of seasoned fries and a generous serving of fried chicken pops, estimated at approximately 450 g total.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken nuggets, frozen, unprepared (USDA SR Legacy 21053) and French fries, fast food (USDA SR Legacy 21138) — composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct nutritional entry exists for Potato Corner Tera Chicken Pops Combo in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values are estimated as a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for fast-food fried chicken bites (approx. 50% by weight, USDA FDC 21053 and related fried chicken entries) and fast-food seasoned French fries (approx. 50% by weight, USDA FDC 21138). Per-100g values are blended proportionally. Seasoning flavouring adds minor sodium contribution estimated from USDA seasoning data. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — French fries and fried potato products (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Potato Corner Tera Chicken Pops Combo. GI of 65 is estimated as a weighted average of fast-food French fries (GI ~63–75, University of Sydney GI Database) and fried chicken bites (GI ~40–50, low due to protein and fat content slowing glucose absorption). Weighted 50:50 by mass yields an estimated composite GI of approximately 65 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 132.75 g carbohydrate in 450 g serving) / 100 ≈ 86.3; however, using per-100g carbohydrate of 29.5 g × 4.5 portions = 132.75 g total carbohydrate, GL = 65 × 132.75 / 100 = 86.3. Reported GL per serving is 86.3 rounded to 86.3. Note: GL recalculated as 65 × (29.5 × 450/100) / 100 = 65 × 132.75 / 100 = 86.3.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from seasoning and batter coating
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this combo meal, as the estimated 420 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 1890 mg per full serving) substantially contributes to the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, and the seasoning flavouring further elevates sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load from deep-fried potato fries and battered chicken coating
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this combo meal only occasionally and in small portions, as the estimated glycemic load of approximately 86 per full serving and the high refined carbohydrate content from fries and batter are likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium from deep-frying and seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this combo meal due to its saturated fat content (approximately 3.2 g per 100 g), presence of trans fat from deep-frying, and high sodium load, all of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Tera Chicken Pops Combo is a large fast-food combo meal sold at Potato Corner outlets in Singapore, notably at the Funan mall location. It consists of two components: a generous portion of deep-fried seasoned French fries and a serving of fried chicken pops (small boneless fried chicken bites), each accompanied by a choice of flavouring seasoning powder. The combo is marketed as the largest and most filling option in the Potato Corner lineup, positioned above the Mega combo. The chicken pops are coated in a seasoned batter and deep-fried until crispy, yielding a crunchy exterior with tender, juicy chicken inside. The fries are Potato Corner's signature crinkle-cut or straight-cut fries, deep-fried and dusted with one of several proprietary seasoning flavours such as BBQ, cheese, sour cream, or chilli. The meal is high in energy (approximately 285 kcal per 100 g), with significant fat content from deep-frying and moderate protein from the chicken component. The sodium content is elevated due to seasoning powders, making this meal a concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per full serving is high, driven primarily by the potato fries. This combo is a popular snack or light meal choice in Singapore's fast-food and mall food-court culture, particularly among younger consumers. It is classified as ultra-processed due to the batter coating, seasoning additives, and deep-frying preparation method.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for Potato Corner Tera Chicken Pops Combo was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for fast-food fried chicken bites and fast-food French fries. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for French fries and fried chicken. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation and absence of a direct product-specific source.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite sources used for this estimation. All other micronutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy weighted composite. Serving size of 450 g is an estimate based on typical fast-food combo meal weights for a large combo (fries + chicken bites); the actual Potato Corner Tera combo weight is not publicly disclosed. Glycemic load per serving recalculated: GL = GI (65) × carbohydrate per serving (29.5 g/100g × 450 g / 100) / 100 = 65 × 132.75 / 100 = 86.3; the value 86.3 is reported in glycemic_load_per_serving field corrected below. Halal status is assumed based on Potato Corner Singapore's general halal certification status; consumers should verify current certification. The food_name has been normalised from the promotional menu name 'Tera Chicken Pops Combo' to the canonical descriptive name 'Chicken Pops and Fries Combo'; the original menu name is preserved in aliases.
